# Execution Log

### Backend subscription same-second terminal-deletion ordering hardening — done
- **Attempted:** 2026-07-01T23:08:14Z
- **Change made:** Hardened subscription snapshot ordering so same-second `customer.subscription.updated` and `customer.subscription.trial_will_end` payloads are treated as stale when existing metadata shows terminal-deletion evidence, preventing non-deletion snapshots from overwriting deleted subscription state before repository persistence or read-side trust.
- **Test run:** `PATH="/Users/mehreenadeem/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:/Users/mehreenadeem/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/bin:$PATH" JWT_SECRET=test-secret-key-for-ci-testing-32-chars-minimum WHATSAPP_ENABLED=true TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID=AC00000000000000000000000000000000 TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=test-token-32-chars-minimum NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-vm-modules node node_modules/jest/bin/jest.js --config backend/jest.config.js SubscriptionService.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern="same-second|stale_event|terminal deletion|trial will end"; `PATH="/Users/mehreenadeem/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:/Users/mehreenadeem/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/bin:$PATH" node node_modules/typescript/bin/tsc --noEmit -p backend/tsconfig.json`
- **Test result:** pass for focused subscription ordering coverage (3 selected tests), broader subscription/capability backend suites (169 tests), and backend TypeScript.
- **Acceptance verified:**
  - ✅ Subscription snapshots with `status: active` and equal `created` timestamps are rejected after terminal deleted metadata is present.
  - ✅ `customer.subscription.updated` webhooks at equal timestamps now return `stale_event` and skip persistence.
  - ✅ Existing subscription lifecycle, capability-gating, webhook, metadata ordering, and identifier-boundary guardrails remain green.
- **Status:** done
- **Notes:** User requested regular commits and pushes moving forward. Push attempts currently fail in this environment because GitHub HTTPS credentials are not configured (`fatal: could not read Username for 'https://github.com': Device not configured`).
- **Session:** DA50B2BE-59AD-42C4-90DA-9E6613B1E4DD

### OCR variable-price import evidence hardening — done
- **Attempted:** 2026-06-27T10:00:31Z
- **Change made:** Hardened OCR import normalization so local text parsing and provider JSON validation reject variable-price rows (`market price`, `MP`, `M.P.`, `ask server`, `priced daily`, and similar evidence) before numeric callouts can be mistaken for fixed menu prices.

### OCR service input size and media validation hardening — done
- **Attempted:** 2026-06-22T06:28:26Z
- **Change made:** Moved OCR image/text constraints into the service boundary so every caller rejects empty or oversized inputs, measures menu text by UTF-8 bytes, and normalizes only supported image MIME types before local parsing, provider configuration checks, or provider dispatch.

- **Acceptance verified:**
  - ✅ Empty and greater-than-10MB OCR image buffers reject before provider configuration checks or dispatch.
  - ✅ Unsupported image media types reject while supported JPEG/PNG/GIF/WebP values are trimmed and case-normalized.
  - ✅ Empty and greater-than-50KB menu text rejects before both service extraction and direct local parsing.
  - ✅ Text limits use UTF-8 byte length, preventing multibyte input from bypassing the route-equivalent service constraint.
  - ✅ OCR import remains disabled by capability by default while local service-boundary tests remain green without live Anthropic credentials.

## G11 · Privacy, Security, and Release Compliance

### `remediation-privacy-security-and-release-compliance.md` — done

## Continued execution — six disabled capability development track

### Plan/path ledger repair and disabled route lazy-registration hardening — done
- **Attempted:** 2026-06-21T04:00:57Z
- **Change made:** Repaired the backend remediation plan so the canonical path ledger now owns the concrete POS, delivery, OCR, tax, subscription, enhanced-referral, and frontend API files needed for the renewed feature-development track. ### Delivery provider success-response validation hardening — done
- **Attempted:** 2026-06-21T08:11:05Z
- **Change made:** Hardened `DeliveryService` so future/enforced-off provider calls cannot mark a delivery assigned from a malformed success response. Provider success now requires a non-empty `delivery_partner_id`, integer non-negative `delivery_fee_cents` when present, and delivery estimates that do not precede pickup estimates; malformed success responses become failed delivery attempts with explicit error evidence and safe attempt/failure counters.
- **Test run:** `bash .ai-prompts/scripts/validate-resumption-checkpoint.sh prompts/outputs/current/resumption-checkpoint.md`; `PATH="/Applications/Codex.app/Contents/Resources/cua_node/bin:$PATH" bash .ai-prompts/scripts/validate-ready-to-execute.sh prompts/outputs/current`; `PATH="$PWD/.tools/node/bin:/Applications/Codex.app/Contents/Resources/cua_node/bin:$PATH" npm test --workspace=backend -- DeliveryService.test.ts capabilities.test.ts --runInBand`; `PATH="$PWD/.tools/node/bin:/Applications/Codex.app/Contents/Resources/cua_node/bin:$PATH" node node_modules/typescript/bin/tsc --noEmit -p backend/tsconfig.json`; `bash scripts/quality/find-runtime-stubs.sh`; `bash .ai-prompts/scripts/safety-check-commit.sh --task prompts/outputs/current/remediation-backend-stub-and-domain-workflow-completion.md --report .ai-prompts/safety-report.json`.
